name: Lupe Castillo
data_note: Limited public information available. Check your county clerk's office or the Illinois State Board of Elections for filing info.
bio: Lupe Castillo is a community activist from Little Village (40 years) and perennial Republican candidate in the heavily Democratic IL-04 district. She is the sole Republican candidate in the IL-04 primary. She has run twice against Chuy Garcia (2022: 28.1%, 2024: 27.3%). Born in San Antonio; Spanish is her first language; associate degree from Washburne Community Trade School. Trump-aligned platform. (Source: Ballotpedia 2024 survey; ILSOS filing confirmed)
prior_office: Candidate for IL-04 (2022, 2024); Candidate for IL House District 23 (withdrew 2024)

jacket.note: FEC candidate totals pending ETL import.

career_history.org: 10th CPD District Advisory Committee; Little Village Community Council; Southwest Side GOP
career_history.highlight: 40 years in Little Village; born in San Antonio TX; perennial candidate in D+17 district

policy_platform.position: Border security and immigration reform; finish the border wall; no sanctuary cities; overhaul the asylum process.
Support law enforcement; oppose defunding. Strong crime reduction focus.
No more taxes on Social Security, tips, or overtime. Cut government spending. Energy independence.
Strong support for Israel. NATO burden-sharing for Ukraine.
